channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Talking to Yourself: Defying Forgetting in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2602.20162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Catastrophic forgetting remains a major challenge when fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) on narrow, task-specific data, often degrading their general knowledge and reasoning abilities. We propose SA-SFT, a lightweight self-augmentation routine ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, reasoning, analysis, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ConceptRM: The Quest to Mitigate Alert Fatigue through Consensus-Based Purity-Driven Data Cleaning for Reflection Modelling
item.description: arXiv:2602.20166v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In many applications involving intelligent agents, the overwhelming volume of alerts (mostly false) generated by the agents may desensitize users and cause them to overlook critical issues, leading to the so-called ''alert fatigue''. A common strategy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, product, LLM, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What Makes a Good Query? Measuring the Impact of Human-Confusing Linguistic Features on LLM Performance
item.description: arXiv:2602.20300v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations are usually treated as defects of the model or its decoding strategy. Drawing on classical linguistics, we argue that a query's form can also shape a listener's (and model's) response. We operationalize this i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, vector, analysis, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Natural Language Processing Models for Robust Document Categorization
item.description: arXiv:2602.20336v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This article presents an evaluation of several machine learning methods applied to automated text classification, alongside the design of a demonstrative system for unbalanced document categorization and distribution. The study focuses on balancing cl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, context, RAG, ICL, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How communicatively optimal are exact numeral systems? Once more on lexicon size and morphosyntactic complexity
item.description: arXiv:2602.20372v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent research argues that exact recursive numeral systems optimize communicative efficiency by balancing a tradeoff between the size of the numeral lexicon and the average morphosyntactic complexity (roughly length in morphemes) of numeral terms. We...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, research, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Disentangling Geometry, Performance, and Training in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2602.20433v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geometric properties of Transformer weights, particularly the unembedding matrix, have been widely useful in language model interpretability research. Yet, their utility for estimating downstream performance remains unclear. In this work, we systemati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, embedding, analysis, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DMCD: Semantic-Statistical Framework for Causal Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2602.20333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present DMCD (DataMap Causal Discovery), a two-phase causal discovery framework that integrates LLM-based semantic drafting from variable metadata with statistical validation on observational data. In Phase I, a large language model proposes a spar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, analysis, framework, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PreScience: A Benchmark for Forecasting Scientific Contributions
item.description: arXiv:2602.20459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can AI systems trained on the scientific record up to a fixed point in time forecast the scientific advances that follow? Such a capability could help researchers identify collaborators and impactful research directions, and anticipate which problems ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, paper, product, RAG, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: KairosVL: Orchestrating Time Series and Semantics for Unified Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2602.20494v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Driven by the increasingly complex and decision-oriented demands of time series analysis, we introduce the Semantic-Conditional Time Series Reasoning task, which extends conventional time series analysis beyond purely numerical modeling to incorporate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, analysis, reasoning, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Inner Speech as Behavior Guides: Steerable Imitation of Diverse Behaviors for Human-AI coordination
item.description: arXiv:2602.20517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effective human-AI coordination requires artificial agents capable of exhibiting and responding to human-like behaviors while adapting to changing contexts. Imitation learning has emerged as one of the prominent approaches to build such agents by trai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, demonstration, framework, vision, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MoBiQuant: Mixture-of-Bits Quantization for Token-Adaptive Elastic LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2602.20191v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Changing runtime complexity on cloud and edge devices necessitates elastic large language model (LLM) deployment, where an LLM can be inferred with various quantization precisions based on available computational resources. However, it has been observ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, framework, LLM, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FedAvg-Based CTMC Hazard Model for Federated Bridge Deterioration Assessment
item.description: arXiv:2602.20194v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bridge periodic inspection records contain sensitive information about public infrastructure, making cross-organizational data sharing impractical under existing data governance constraints. We propose a federated framework for estimating a Continuous...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, platform, vector, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Controllable Exploration in Hybrid-Policy RLVR for Multi-Modal Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2602.20197v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a primary learning paradigm for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of multi-modal large language models (MLLMs). However, during RL training, the enormous state space of MLLM and s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, paper, product, reasoning, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Golden Layers and Where to Find Them: Improved Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models Via Layer Gradient Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2602.20207v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge editing in Large Language Models (LLMs) aims to update the model's prediction for a specific query to a desired target while preserving its behavior on all other inputs. This process typically involves two stages: identifying the layer to ed...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, analysis, LLM, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Model Merging in the Essential Subspace
item.description: arXiv:2602.20208v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Model merging aims to integrate multiple task-specific fine-tuned models derived from a shared pre-trained checkpoint into a single multi-task model without additional training. Despite extensive research, task interference remains a major obstacle th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, analysis, framework, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: KnapSpec: Self-Speculative Decoding via Adaptive Layer Selection as a Knapsack Problem
item.description: arXiv:2602.20217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-speculative decoding (SSD) accelerates LLM inference by skipping layers to create an efficient draft model, yet existing methods often rely on static heuristics that ignore the dynamic computational overhead of attention in long-context scenarios...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, attention, analysis, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multimodal Crystal Flow: Any-to-Any Modality Generation for Unified Crystal Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2602.20210v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Crystal modeling spans a family of conditional and unconditional generation tasks across different modalities, including crystal structure prediction (CSP) and \emph{de novo} generation (DNG). While recent deep generative models have shown promising p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, framework, multimodal, template, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Benchmarking Distilled Language Models: Performance and Efficiency in Resource-Constrained Settings
item.description: arXiv:2602.20164v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation offers a transformative pathway to developing powerful, yet efficient, small language models (SLMs) suitable for resource-constrained environments. In this paper, we benchmark the performance and computational cost of distilled ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, paper, reasoning, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multilevel Determinants of Overweight and Obesity Among U.S. Children Aged 10-17: Comparative Evaluation of Statistical and Machine Learning Approaches Using the 2021 National Survey of Children's Health
item.description: arXiv:2602.20303v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background: Childhood and adolescent overweight and obesity remain major public health concerns in the United States and are shaped by behavioral, household, and community factors. Their joint predictive structure at the population level remains incom...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Diffusion Modulation via Environment Mechanism Modeling for Planning
item.description: arXiv:2602.20422v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion models have shown promising capabilities in trajectory generation for planning in offline reinforcement learning (RL). However, conventional diffusion-based planning methods often fail to account for the fact that generating trajectories in ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
